source.filename: Tinea pedis : Athletes Foot (chronic - fissuring) DST-044.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Allergic contact dermatitis (candidate for footwear contact dermatitis)
Candidiasis of skin (candidate for candidal intertrigo)

transform_flags: Age inferred as adult (~42) — the source note states no age; body weight 85 kg, back-to-back long work shifts and heavy occlusive work boots establish a working-age adult. Please confirm on clinician review.

Sex inferred as male with a WEAK basis in the source note (heavy enclosed work boots and long back-to-back manual shifts) — recorded because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation/correction. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Geographic context not stated in the source — omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician (home visit) who inspected the foot, palpated for ascending cellulitis and lymphadenopathy, measured vitals/weight and cleansed/dried the web space. Patient/video-observable findings (white macerated web-space skin, the deep weeping fissure, moccasin plantar scaling, hobbling, self-reported no fever/no ascending streaks) routed to 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician-measured vitals (BP 128/82, HR 88, Temp 37.1, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 85 kg) and the un-palpable early-cellulitis/lymphadenopathy question sealed in 10/11 with telehealth_limits. The clinician's cleanse/dry step was reprojected to patient-performed drying and cream application.

De-anchoring applied: the source filename/header name the diagnosis (tinea pedis / athlete's foot); the green side (01/02) carries only the lay presentation (long-standing itchy flaky soggy skin between the toes, a deep painful weeping split, dry flaky soles, sweaty occlusive work boots) and no diagnosis label. The full primary_diagnosis.name does not appear in any injectable field.

Tier set to T2 (prompt in-person GP review), NOT an urgent/emergency tier: interdigital tinea pedis is a genuine T1-T2 dermatology complaint and the source's own cellulitis red-flag screen is negative (afebrile, no ascending erythema, no purulence, no lymphadenopathy) with a disposition of topical creams + routine GP review in 7 days. The open weeping fissure (portal of entry) justifies prompt in-person review over pure self-care and drives prominent mandatory secondary-infection safety-net edges (T4 ascending cellulitis / T5 necrotising infection or sepsis) — but not a baseline uplift. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass set to T1 so a correct recognise + topical antifungal + drying + explicit cellulitis safety-net + review answer PASSES. Reflexive escalation to 000/ED and pure self-care with no follow-up/safety-net are both scored as errors (over- and under-triage).
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00244 — maintainer to confirm/reassign the SEQ within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket via ingest --reseq.

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes recorded for the primary diagnosis, the exacerbating hyperhidrosis and the differentials with verification_status unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; none claimed verified. Several (hyperhidrosis, footwear contact dermatitis, pompholyx) are best-effort candidates for later terminology-receipt confirmation.

Drug AMT/PBS codes for miconazole 2%/hydrocortisone 1% cream, terbinafine 1% cream and antifungal foot powder were NOT assigned (candidate pending terminology receipt) rather than fabricated; amt_snomed_code omitted on the medication objects.

Doses (miconazole 2%/hydrocortisone 1% cream BD to the fissure for 3 days; terbinafine 1% cream once daily to sole and web spaces for 2-4 weeks) taken verbatim from the source note; none synthesised. Adult patient — no paediatric dosing issue. Oral antifungal marked second_line_if_first_fails (not prescribed by the source; reserved for topical failure/extensive disease).

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
